Identification.LongTitle: An Act to amend the Canada Labour Code (hours of work of flight attendants)

Provision.Text: This enactment amends the Canada Labour Code to provide that, in calculating the time in respect of which an employee who is a flight attendant is to be paid, the employer must include the time that the employee spends in carrying out their pre-flight and post-flight duties and in completing mandatory training programs.
His Majesty, by and with the advice and consent of the Senate and House of Commons of Canada, enacts as follows:

Section.Text: This Act may be cited as the Fairness for Flight Attendants Act.
Section 169 of the Canada Labour Code is amended by adding the following after subsection (1):

Subsection.MarginalNote: Calculation of hours of work â flight attendants
Subsection.Text: In calculating the time worked in a day or in a week, as the case may be, for which an employee who is a flight attendant is to be paid, the employer must include the time that the employee spends in

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: carrying out all pre-flight and post-flight duties relating to aircraft security and passenger service, including assisting with embarking and disembarking and pre-flight cabin and passenger safety checks;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: attending at the work place at the call of the employer and at the disposal of the employer, including during a flight delay, whether or not the delay is within the employerâs control.

Section.Text: The Act is amended by adding the following after section 181:

Section.MarginalNote: Hours of work of flight attendants
Section.Text: An order or regulation must not be made under this Part that has the effect of limiting the application of subsection 169(1.1).
